compiled_state.description:
# Make the pre-commit lint/typecheck hooks venv-independent

## What
`.pre-commit-config.yaml` runs `make lint`/`make typecheck` as `language: system`, so when the venv is not activated the hook resolves a wrong `ruff` (newer, F811-strict) from PATH and cannot find `mypy` — the local commit gate fails and forces `--no-verify` (which also skips the Gerrit Change-Id hook). Make the Makefile targets invoke the venv-pinned tools explicitly (e.g. `python -m ruff`/`python -m mypy` or `.venv/bin/…`) so the hook works regardless of activation. No real code defect exists (pinned ruff 0.15.19 = CI is clean).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `make lint` + `make typecheck` (and the pre-commit hook) resolve the pinned venv tools without requiring `source .venv/bin/activate`.
- [ ] A commit in a fresh shell (venv not pre-activated) runs the hook to completion (no wrong-ruff/no-mypy failure).
- [ ] Commit carries a `rebar-ticket:` trailer.
compiled_state.title: Make the pre-commit lint/typecheck hooks venv-independent
